text: Agile methodology is a popular approach in software development. Work is divided into short phases called sprints, which usually last two to four weeks. Each day, the team holds a brief standup meeting to discuss progress and any problems. Before a sprint begins, the team chooses tasks from a backlog, which is a list of work that still needs to be done. These tasks are written as user stories, simple descriptions of a feature from a customer's perspective. This structure supports iterative development, meaning the product is improved each sprint based on feedback, helping teams adapt quickly to changes.
